title: Applied Cryptographic Engineering in Go 1.26
description: Go developers who can build and test ordinary programs and need to select, implement, verify, migrate, and operate cryptographic systems without inventing cryptography or overstating evidence. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate go 1.26.5 and x/crypto v0.54.0; threat modeling; bytes and canonical formats; randomness; secret lifecycle; classical defect labs; aes; modes; aead; hashes; hmac; hkdf; password hashing; modular reasoning; rsa; x25519; ed25519 and ecdsa; hpke; envelope encryption; key management; x.509; tls 1.3; secure protocol state machines; encrypted file formats; ml-kem, ml-dsa, and slh-dsa migration; misuse resistance; vectors, negative tests, fuzzing and interoperability; fips boundaries; operations, incidents, recovery, and release defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-go security decision functions and allowlisted cryptographic api exercises plus explicit go 1.26.5 native compile, os entropy, native timing, vector, fuzz, race, interop, tls, hsm/kms, certificate, load, fault, compliance, recovery, and production transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative authentic cryptographic systems and a performance-based production defense examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves Go correctness, canonical bytes, explicit threat and trust boundaries, purpose-separated keys, bounded attacker-controlled work, uniform safe failures, secret-safe observability, negative tests, and evidence limits before adding one cryptographic boundary.

Browser Go is deterministic and isolated. It models byte identity, formats, nonce and key state, policies, protocol transitions, attacks, migration, and evidence decisions and runs only an allowlisted side-effect-free cryptographic API subset with fixed fixtures. It never generates production keys, proves entropy or constant time, opens networks, contacts TLS, HSM/KMS or certificate systems, reads host secrets, executes commands, or causes external effects.

Passing work requires stable scenario and artifact identity, a named asset and adversary, exact security property, canonical inputs, versioned algorithm and parameter policy, key/nonce ownership, bounded work, observable success and failure, tamper and changed-case evidence, recovery, and explicit native or production transfer limits.

competencies.statement: Define confidentiality, integrity, authenticity, freshness, forward secrecy, availability, privacy, and accountability as separate observable outcomes.
misconceptions: Encryption automatically supplies every security property.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define confidentiality, integrity, authenticity, freshness, forward secrecy, availability, privacy, and accountability as separate observable outcomes.

competencies.statement: Model assets, actors, capabilities, entry points, trust boundaries, abuse cases, and acceptable residual risk before choosing a primitive.
misconceptions: Selecting AES first is an adequate threat model.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model assets, actors, capabilities, entry points, trust boundaries, abuse cases, and acceptable residual risk before choosing a primitive.

competencies.statement: Separate mathematical primitive claims, API behavior, implementation evidence, deployment controls, compliance status, and stakeholder outcomes.
misconceptions: A passing encrypt-decrypt round trip proves production security.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate mathematical primitive claims, API behavior, implementation evidence, deployment controls, compliance status, and stakeholder outcomes.

competencies.statement: Apply Kerckhoffs principle and choose reviewed standard-library or maintained cryptographic APIs instead of secret algorithms.
misconceptions: An unpublished algorithm is secure because attackers cannot inspect it.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply Kerckhoffs principle and choose reviewed standard-library or maintained cryptographic APIs instead of secret algorithms.

competencies.statement: Bound cryptographic testing by authorization, data minimization, export, privacy, disclosure, and incident duties.
misconceptions: A security learning objective authorizes testing any reachable system.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound cryptographic testing by authorization, data minimization, export, privacy, disclosure, and incident duties.

competencies.statement: Trace strings, UTF-8, bytes, hexadecimal, base64, binary fields, lengths, and canonical encodings without changing their security meaning.
misconceptions: Encoding bytes makes them confidential.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace strings, UTF-8, bytes, hexadecimal, base64, binary fields, lengths, and canonical encodings without changing their security meaning.

competencies.statement: Distinguish encoding, checksums, hashing, MACs, signatures, encryption, key agreement, KEMs, and password hashing by purpose and reversibility.
misconceptions: Hashing, encryption, and encoding are interchangeable transforms.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish encoding, checksums, hashing, MACs, signatures, encryption, key agreement, KEMs, and password hashing by purpose and reversibility.

competencies.statement: Choose a vetted high-level construction that supplies the required combined properties instead of composing low-level primitives casually.
misconceptions: Combining individually secure primitives always produces a secure protocol.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Choose a vetted high-level construction that supplies the required combined properties instead of composing low-level primitives casually.

competencies.statement: Assign unambiguous labels, contexts, versions, roles, and byte encodings so one cryptographic use cannot be replayed as another.
misconceptions: Reusing one digest or key across unrelated purposes is harmless when lengths match.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Assign unambiguous labels, contexts, versions, roles, and byte encodings so one cryptographic use cannot be replayed as another.

competencies.statement: Define injective length-delimited serialization and reject ambiguous or non-canonical inputs before signing or authenticating.
misconceptions: Concatenating fields without boundaries produces one unique signed message.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define injective length-delimited serialization and reject ambiguous or non-canonical inputs before signing or authenticating.

competencies.statement: Use current Go cryptographic randomness APIs for keys, tokens, salts, nonces, and blinding while handling failure according to the API contract.
misconceptions: math/rand becomes cryptographically secure when seeded frequently.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use current Go cryptographic randomness APIs for keys, tokens, salts, nonces, and blinding while handling failure according to the API contract.

competencies.statement: Distinguish entropy sources, deterministic random bit generators, unpredictability, uniqueness, bias, and statistical appearance.
misconceptions: A sequence that looks random is necessarily unpredictable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish entropy sources, deterministic random bit generators, unpredictability, uniqueness, bias, and statistical appearance.

competencies.statement: Generate algorithm-compatible keys at supported strengths and retain provenance, purpose, owner, and creation evidence.
misconceptions: Any random byte string is a valid key for every algorithm.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Generate algorithm-compatible keys at supported strengths and retain provenance, purpose, owner, and creation evidence.

competencies.statement: Enforce construction-specific nonce uniqueness or misuse limits across concurrency, restart, rollback, and replication.
misconceptions: A nonce must always be secret but may repeat.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Enforce construction-specific nonce uniqueness or misuse limits across concurrency, restart, rollback, and replication.

competencies.statement: Separate public unique salts, unpredictable bearer tokens, nonces, IVs, seeds, and secret keys by their exact guarantees.
misconceptions: Salt, nonce, IV, token, seed, and key are synonyms.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate public unique salts, unpredictable bearer tokens, nonces, IVs, seeds, and secret keys by their exact guarantees.

competencies.statement: Trace secret creation, copies, aliases, use, retention, rotation, destruction limits, logs, crashes, backups, and ownership across Go boundaries.
misconceptions: Calling clear on one slice proves every secret copy is erased.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace secret creation, copies, aliases, use, retention, rotation, destruction limits, logs, crashes, backups, and ownership across Go boundaries.

competencies.statement: Minimize conversions and copies of secret-bearing strings and byte slices while documenting unavoidable runtime limits.
misconceptions: Strings are preferable secret containers because they are immutable.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Minimize conversions and copies of secret-bearing strings and byte slices while documenting unavoidable runtime limits.

competencies.statement: Use constant-time comparison APIs for fixed-length secret-dependent values and reject length or parse errors without secret-dependent detail.
misconceptions: Ordinary byte equality is safe for MACs because network noise hides timing.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use constant-time comparison APIs for fixed-length secret-dependent values and reject length or parse errors without secret-dependent detail.

competencies.statement: Identify timing, cache, branch, allocation, power, fault, error, and length channels and state which are or are not tested.
misconceptions: Using a constant-time compare makes the whole application constant time.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify timing, cache, branch, allocation, power, fault, error, and length channels and state which are or are not tested.

competencies.statement: Design useful logs, metrics, traces, dumps, and incident artifacts that never expose keys, plaintext, password material, tokens, or high-cardinality identities.

competencies.statement: Implement and break a bounded shift or substitution cipher to connect keyspace, language redundancy, frequency evidence, and attacker cost.

competencies.statement: Demonstrate how repeated XOR keystream use reveals relationships between plaintexts without presenting XOR alone as production encryption.
misconceptions: XOR is secure whenever the key bytes are hidden.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Demonstrate how repeated XOR keystream use reveals relationships between plaintexts without presenting XOR alone as production encryption.

competencies.statement: Explain one-time-pad key length, uniformity, secrecy, single use, distribution, and destruction requirements and their operational cost.

competencies.statement: Model offline and online search by keyspace, guess rate, parallelism, detection, lockout, value, and uncertainty without attacking unauthorized targets.
misconceptions: Brute force feasibility is determined by key length alone.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model offline and online search by keyspace, guess rate, parallelism, detection, lockout, value, and uncertainty without attacking unauthorized targets.

competencies.statement: Quarantine toy and obsolete algorithms behind fixtures, negative tests, decryption-only migration, telemetry, deadlines, and deletion criteria.

competencies.statement: Explain block-cipher keyed permutation behavior and why messages require an approved mode or higher-level construction.
misconceptions: Encrypting each block independently preserves message confidentiality.

competencies.statement: Validate key and block lengths before using low-level AES APIs and convert panics or errors into bounded caller behavior.
misconceptions: The AES API safely truncates keys and partial blocks.

competencies.statement: Use authoritative known-answer vectors to verify byte order, exact inputs, exact outputs, and implementation wiring without treating vectors as a security proof.
misconceptions: An encrypt-decrypt round trip detects every incorrect AES implementation.

competencies.statement: Distinguish algorithm approval, implementation selection, hardware acceleration, side-channel assumptions, and Go FIPS module status.
misconceptions: Using AES automatically means the application is FIPS compliant.

competencies.statement: Demonstrate deterministic block-pattern leakage and prohibit ECB for application messages.

competencies.statement: Trace CBC chaining, IV requirements, padding validation, malleability, and oracle behavior as a legacy risk.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace CBC chaining, IV requirements, padding validation, malleability, and oracle behavior as a legacy risk.

competencies.statement: Trace CTR keystream generation and prove that key-counter reuse destroys confidentiality and enables malleation.

competencies.statement: For unavoidable legacy modes, authenticate versioned context and ciphertext before decryption with independent keys and uniform failures.

competencies.statement: Build a bounded decrypt-only adapter that authenticates format, suppresses oracles, records migration, and never emits new legacy ciphertext.

competencies.statement: Design a crash-, concurrency-, rollback-, and replication-safe GCM nonce strategy within the construction limits.

competencies.statement: Collapse attacker-visible authentication failures while retaining safe internal classification and metrics.

masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use ECDSA through current Go APIs with approved curves, supported hashes, canonical encodings, and no custom nonce generation.

competencies.statement: Bind artifact type, version, role, tenant, key ID, expiry, and canonical bytes to prevent cross-protocol signature reuse.
misconceptions: Signing a display string safely authenticates every structured interpretation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind artifact type, version, role, tenant, key ID, expiry, and canonical bytes to prevent cross-protocol signature reuse.

competencies.statement: Apply threshold, authorization, revocation, expiry, transparency, and audit policy after cryptographic verification.
misconceptions: Cryptographic verification is the final authorization decision.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Apply threshold, authorization, revocation, expiry, transparency, and audit policy after cryptographic verification.

competencies.statement: Combine public-key key establishment with symmetric AEAD for bounded scalable payload encryption and explicit recipient identity.
misconceptions: Public-key encryption should process the entire large payload directly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine public-key key establishment with symmetric AEAD for bounded scalable payload encryption and explicit recipient identity.

competencies.statement: Use Go HPKE suites and modes with exact KEM, KDF, AEAD, info, associated data, key encoding, and one-shot or context lifecycle.
misconceptions: HPKE authenticates sender identity in every mode automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Go HPKE suites and modes with exact KEM, KDF, AEAD, info, associated data, key encoding, and one-shot or context lifecycle.

competencies.statement: Generate per-object data keys, wrap them under recipient or key-encryption keys, authenticate metadata, and separate data from key custody.
misconceptions: Encrypting a DEK means its wrapping key may be stored beside it without concern.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Generate per-object data keys, wrap them under recipient or key-encryption keys, authenticate metadata, and separate data from key custody.

competencies.statement: Bind each recipient, wrapped key, suite, object version, and payload digest so entries cannot be swapped or replayed.
misconceptions: All recipients may safely share one unauthenticated wrapped-key list.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind each recipient, wrapped key, suite, object version, and payload digest so entries cannot be swapped or replayed.

competencies.statement: Rotate recipient or KEK protection by authenticated rewrapping while preserving payload identity, audit, rollback, and revocation limits.
misconceptions: Rewrapping a data key revokes every old copy of that key.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Rotate recipient or KEK protection by authenticated rewrapping while preserving payload identity, audit, rollback, and revocation limits.

competencies.statement: Define generation, registration, activation, distribution, use, suspension, rotation, archival, recovery, revocation, destruction, and evidence ownership.
misconceptions: Key management begins and ends with generating random bytes.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define generation, registration, activation, distribution, use, suspension, rotation, archival, recovery, revocation, destruction, and evidence ownership.

competencies.statement: Separate immutable key version, stable logical key ID, mutable alias, algorithm, purpose, state, and cryptoperiod.
misconceptions: A key alias is immutable evidence of which key protected data.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate immutable key version, stable logical key ID, mutable alias, algorithm, purpose, state, and cryptoperiod.

competencies.statement: Grant narrow sign, verify, encrypt, decrypt, wrap, unwrap, derive, rotate, export, and administer capabilities across separate identities.
misconceptions: A service that can encrypt must also be allowed to decrypt and export keys.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Grant narrow sign, verify, encrypt, decrypt, wrap, unwrap, derive, rotate, export, and administer capabilities across separate identities.

competencies.statement: Design an interface for non-exportable HSM or KMS operations and state what attestation, latency, quota, availability, and audit evidence remains external.
misconceptions: Using a cloud KMS proves keys can never be exposed.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design an interface for non-exportable HSM or KMS operations and state what attestation, latency, quota, availability, and audit evidence remains external.

competencies.statement: Inventory affected uses, contain authority, rotate or revoke, rewrap or re-encrypt, restore service, notify stakeholders, and prove old-key handling.
misconceptions: Deleting the suspected key immediately is always the safest first response.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inventory affected uses, contain authority, rotate or revoke, rewrap or re-encrypt, restore service, notify stakeholders, and prove old-key handling.

competencies.statement: Parse certificates, names, SANs, key usages, extensions, serials, validity, signatures, and chains without treating parsed data as trusted.
misconceptions: A certificate that parses successfully is valid for every purpose.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Parse certificates, names, SANs, key usages, extensions, serials, validity, signatures, and chains without treating parsed data as trusted.

competencies.statement: Build and verify a path to an explicit trust anchor with DNS name, time, basic constraints, key usage, policy, and algorithm checks.
misconceptions: Verifying a certificate signature alone verifies the full chain and hostname.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Build and verify a path to an explicit trust anchor with DNS name, time, basic constraints, key usage, policy, and algorithm checks.

competencies.statement: Separate system, application, private, tenant, and test roots and prevent accidental trust expansion.
misconceptions: Adding a root for one service safely trusts it for every application.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Separate system, application, private, tenant, and test roots and prevent accidental trust expansion.

competencies.statement: Model short lifetimes, OCSP, CRLs, stapling, soft-fail or hard-fail policy, caching, privacy, and offline behavior.
misconceptions: Revocation guarantees immediate rejection of every compromised certificate.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model short lifetimes, OCSP, CRLs, stapling, soft-fail or hard-fail policy, caching, privacy, and offline behavior.

competencies.statement: Issue bounded leaf certificates with unique serials, correct SANs, usages, profiles, audit, renewal, and protected CA keys.
misconceptions: Copying one certificate template is sufficient for servers, clients, and CAs.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Issue bounded leaf certificates with unique serials, correct SANs, usages, profiles, audit, renewal, and protected CA keys.

competencies.statement: Trace TLS negotiation, key exchange, certificate or PSK authentication, transcript binding, record protection, resumption, and closure.
misconceptions: TLS only encrypts bytes and has no integrity or peer-authentication role.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Trace TLS negotiation, key exchange, certificate or PSK authentication, transcript binding, record protection, resumption, and closure.

competencies.statement: Configure current Go TLS defaults, minimum versions, certificates, roots, names, ALPN, timeouts, and safe verification hooks without insecure skip shortcuts.
misconceptions: InsecureSkipVerify is acceptable when a custom hostname check happens later.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Configure current Go TLS defaults, minimum versions, certificates, roots, names, ALPN, timeouts, and safe verification hooks without insecure skip shortcuts.

competencies.statement: Model tickets, PSKs, rotation, replay, forward secrecy, and application idempotency before enabling resumption or early data.
misconceptions: TLS 1.3 early data has the same replay protection as ordinary application data.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Model tickets, PSKs, rotation, replay, forward secrecy, and application idempotency before enabling resumption or early data.

competencies.statement: Understand Go 1.26 hybrid post-quantum key-exchange defaults, compatibility controls, size/latency effects, downgrade evidence, and rollout.
misconceptions: A hybrid TLS key exchange makes certificates and all application data quantum-safe forever.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Understand Go 1.26 hybrid post-quantum key-exchange defaults, compatibility controls, size/latency effects, downgrade evidence, and rollout.

competencies.statement: Verify handshakes, chains, names, protocol, cipher, exporter, failure, rotation, resumption, and load in controlled environments.
misconceptions: A unit test over net.Pipe proves public-network TLS deployment behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Verify handshakes, chains, names, protocol, cipher, exporter, failure, rotation, resumption, and load in controlled environments.

competencies.statement: Define initiator, responder, identities, trust assumptions, authentication goals, secrecy goals, compromise model, and terminal states.
misconceptions: Choosing strong primitives makes an informal message flow secure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define initiator, responder, identities, trust assumptions, authentication goals, secrecy goals, compromise model, and terminal states.

competencies.statement: Bind ordered message type, role, version, suite, identities, ephemeral values, session ID, sequence, and prior transcript into each state transition.
misconceptions: Encrypting each message prevents reordering and cross-session replay.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bind ordered message type, role, version, suite, identities, ephemeral values, session ID, sequence, and prior transcript into each state transition.

competencies.statement: Use nonces, counters, windows, durable identities, expiry, and idempotent effects according to channel and availability constraints.
misconceptions: Timestamps alone prevent replay in distributed systems.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use nonces, counters, windows, durable identities, expiry, and idempotent effects according to channel and availability constraints.

competencies.statement: Authenticate offered and selected versions, algorithms, parameters, and features and fail closed on unsupported or stripped choices.
misconceptions: Allowing both modern and legacy suites cannot weaken modern peers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Authenticate offered and selected versions, algorithms, parameters, and features and fail closed on unsupported or stripped choices.

competencies.statement: Use attack trees, state exploration, test vectors, interop peers, and formal tools as distinct evidence with explicit model limitations.
misconceptions: One protocol diagram and code review prove resistance to active attackers.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use attack trees, state exploration, test vectors, interop peers, and formal tools as distinct evidence with explicit model limitations.

competencies.statement: Define a bounded versioned file envelope with magic, suite, key reference, KDF parameters, nonce base, chunk policy, metadata, and authenticated lengths.
misconceptions: AEAD makes every surrounding file-format field trustworthy automatically.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define a bounded versioned file envelope with magic, suite, key reference, KDF parameters, nonce base, chunk policy, metadata, and authenticated lengths.

competencies.statement: Derive unique per-chunk nonces and associated data from immutable file identity, epoch, chunk index, length, and final marker.
misconceptions: Incrementing a nonce is safe without bounding chunk count or restart behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Derive unique per-chunk nonces and associated data from immutable file identity, epoch, chunk index, length, and final marker.

competencies.statement: Authenticate chunk position, total or final state, and file identity to reject deletion, duplication, insertion, reordering, and splicing.
misconceptions: Valid independent chunk tags prove the file is complete and ordered.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Authenticate chunk position, total or final state, and file identity to reject deletion, duplication, insertion, reordering, and splicing.

competencies.statement: Buffer or stage plaintext until each chunk and required file-level commitments authenticate, then clean partial outputs on failure.
misconceptions: Streaming decryption may publish every plaintext byte before its tag is checked.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Buffer or stage plaintext until each chunk and required file-level commitments authenticate, then clean partial outputs on failure.

competencies.statement: Support versioned read, controlled rewrite, interrupted migration recovery, key loss behavior, corrupt-media diagnostics, and deletion evidence.
misconceptions: An encrypted backup is recoverable because ciphertext exists in two places.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Support versioned read, controlled rewrite, interrupted migration recovery, key loss behavior, corrupt-media diagnostics, and deletion evidence.

competencies.statement: Inventory long-lived confidentiality, authentication, signatures, roots, protocols, dependencies, data lifetime, and migration lead time.
misconceptions: Quantum migration risk is determined only by when a large quantum computer arrives.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Inventory long-lived confidentiality, authentication, signatures, roots, protocols, dependencies, data lifetime, and migration lead time.

competencies.statement: Use Go ML-KEM interfaces for key encapsulation with strict key/ciphertext parsing, shared-key custody, failure handling, and hybrid context.
misconceptions: ML-KEM encrypts arbitrary application messages directly.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Go ML-KEM interfaces for key encapsulation with strict key/ciphertext parsing, shared-key custody, failure handling, and hybrid context.

competencies.statement: Use Go ML-DSA parameter sets and context-aware signing with explicit key, signature-size, interoperability, and policy tradeoffs.
misconceptions: ML-DSA signatures are drop-in byte-for-byte replacements for Ed25519.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use Go ML-DSA parameter sets and context-aware signing with explicit key, signature-size, interoperability, and policy tradeoffs.

competencies.statement: Explain SLH-DSA standardization, conservative hash-based assumptions, size/performance tradeoffs, and implementation availability limits.
misconceptions: Every NIST post-quantum signature has the same performance and deployment role.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Explain SLH-DSA standardization, conservative hash-based assumptions, size/performance tradeoffs, and implementation availability limits.

competencies.statement: Design hybrid classical-plus-post-quantum rollout, transcript binding, downgrade defense, telemetry, compatibility, rollback, and cryptographic agility.
misconceptions: A hybrid construction is secure if either secret is concatenated without a specified combiner.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Design hybrid classical-plus-post-quantum rollout, transcript binding, downgrade defense, telemetry, compatibility, rollback, and cryptographic agility.

competencies.statement: Identify padding, validity, format, key, user, replay, decompression, and timing oracles across direct and indirect observations.
misconceptions: Returning HTTP 400 for all errors removes every oracle.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Identify padding, validity, format, key, user, replay, decompression, and timing oracles across direct and indirect observations.

competencies.statement: Bound lengths, counts, nesting, KDF work, signature sets, certificate paths, retries, concurrency, memory, CPU, and deadlines before expensive cryptography.
misconceptions: Authenticated cryptographic inputs cannot be used for denial of service.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Bound lengths, counts, nesting, KDF work, signature sets, certificate paths, retries, concurrency, memory, CPU, and deadlines before expensive cryptography.

competencies.statement: Keep useful internal typed causes and safe telemetry while exposing uniform bounded failures and terminal cleanup to untrusted callers.
misconceptions: All cryptographic errors must be discarded, making operations impossible to diagnose.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Keep useful internal typed causes and safe telemetry while exposing uniform bounded failures and terminal cleanup to untrusted callers.

competencies.statement: Analyze compression-before-encryption length leakage, attacker influence, secret proximity, padding limits, and protocol-specific mitigations.
misconceptions: Encryption hides compressed message length.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Analyze compression-before-encryption length leakage, attacker influence, secret proximity, padding limits, and protocol-specific mitigations.

competencies.statement: Move timing, cache, compiler, hardware, co-tenancy, and fault claims to controlled native measurement and specialist review.
misconceptions: Browser timing of a Go interpreter proves production constant-time behavior.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Move timing, cache, compiler, hardware, co-tenancy, and fault claims to controlled native measurement and specialist review.

competencies.statement: Use authoritative known-answer and protocol vectors with provenance, exact bytes, expected successes, expected failures, and stable fixtures.
misconceptions: Random local round trips are stronger than published vectors.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use authoritative known-answer and protocol vectors with provenance, exact bytes, expected successes, expected failures, and stable fixtures.

competencies.statement: Mutate every authenticated field, key, nonce, tag, length, order, version, context, and boundary and require clean rejection without partial effects.
misconceptions: Testing one wrong password sufficiently covers decryption failure.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Mutate every authenticated field, key, nonce, tag, length, order, version, context, and boundary and require clean rejection without partial effects.

competencies.statement: Define properties such as round-trip within valid domains, tamper rejection, parser totality, uniqueness invariants, and version separation without asserting false universal laws.
misconceptions: Property tests can prove cryptographic hardness.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define properties such as round-trip within valid domains, tamper rejection, parser totality, uniqueness invariants, and version separation without asserting false universal laws.

competencies.statement: Fuzz deterministic parsers and state machines with bounded resources, seed corpora, regression artifacts, no global secrets, and reproducible failures.
misconceptions: Fuzzing a cryptographic primitive itself replaces protocol and parser tests.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Fuzz deterministic parsers and state machines with bounded resources, seed corpora, regression artifacts, no global secrets, and reproducible failures.

competencies.statement: Cross-test supported Go versions, architectures, algorithms, parameter sets, external implementations, old data, malformed data, and rollout states.
misconceptions: Passing on one Go version and architecture proves long-term compatibility.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Cross-test supported Go versions, architectures, algorithms, parameter sets, external implementations, old data, malformed data, and rollout states.

competencies.statement: Maintain machine-readable use, purpose, owner, suite, key location, dependency, data lifetime, protocol, version, and migration state for cryptographic assets.
misconceptions: A source search for AES finds the complete cryptographic inventory.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Maintain machine-readable use, purpose, owner, suite, key location, dependency, data lifetime, protocol, version, and migration state for cryptographic assets.

competencies.statement: Centralize policy and versioned suites, authenticate selection, reject unknowns, measure use, retain read compatibility, and remove obsolete write paths.
misconceptions: Supporting many algorithms is the same as cryptographic agility.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Centralize policy and versioned suites, authenticate selection, reject unknowns, measure use, retain read compatibility, and remove obsolete write paths.

competencies.statement: Distinguish approved algorithms, Go cryptographic module version, GOFIPS140 selection, enforcement, validation status, operating environment, and application boundary.
misconceptions: Turning on a FIPS flag certifies the whole application.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Distinguish approved algorithms, Go cryptographic module version, GOFIPS140 selection, enforcement, validation status, operating environment, and application boundary.

competencies.statement: Pin supported Go and x/crypto versions, review release and security notes, run govulncheck, verify reachable findings, and execute compatibility gates.
misconceptions: Cryptographic dependencies should never be updated because changes create risk.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Pin supported Go and x/crypto versions, review release and security notes, run govulncheck, verify reachable findings, and execute compatibility gates.

competencies.statement: Record scoped exception owner, rationale, compensating controls, telemetry, expiry, migration work, approval, and verified closure.
misconceptions: A documented exception may remain permanent if the legacy client is important.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Record scoped exception owner, rationale, compensating controls, telemetry, expiry, migration work, approval, and verified closure.

competencies.statement: Define low-cardinality success, reject, latency, work-factor, key-version, algorithm, expiry, rotation, migration, dependency, and recovery signals without secret leakage.
misconceptions: Cryptographic operations are either correct or failed, so service-level objectives add no value.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Define low-cardinality success, reject, latency, work-factor, key-version, algorithm, expiry, rotation, migration, dependency, and recovery signals without secret leakage.

competencies.statement: Load-test entropy, CPU, memory-hard KDFs, HSM/KMS quotas, certificate services, revocation, key caches, queues, and degraded modes with bounded admission.
misconceptions: Crypto capacity scales linearly with ordinary request throughput.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Load-test entropy, CPU, memory-hard KDFs, HSM/KMS quotas, certificate services, revocation, key caches, queues, and degraded modes with bounded admission.

competencies.statement: Canary reader/writer, algorithm, key, format, certificate, and dependency changes with old/new matrices, dual-read boundaries, telemetry, and rollback.
misconceptions: A successful new-format write proves old readers and rollback remain safe.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Canary reader/writer, algorithm, key, format, certificate, and dependency changes with old/new matrices, dual-read boundaries, telemetry, and rollback.

competencies.statement: Detect, contain, preserve evidence, classify exposure, rotate or revoke, migrate data, restore service, communicate, and verify closure after key or algorithm compromise.
misconceptions: Immediate mass key deletion is always the correct incident response.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect, contain, preserve evidence, classify exposure, rotate or revoke, migrate data, restore service, communicate, and verify closure after key or algorithm compromise.

competencies.statement: Defend threat model, primitive choice, parameters, implementation, key custody, tests, interoperability, side channels, compliance limits, rollout, rollback, recovery, and residual risk before release.
misconceptions: Passing unit tests and a security scan is sufficient release evidence.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Defend threat model, primitive choice, parameters, implementation, key custody, tests, interoperability, side channels, compliance limits, rollout, rollback, recovery, and residual risk before release.

credential.title: Applied Cryptographic Engineering in Go 1.26 Certificate
